subject: Cloud Computing
title: Liquid Cooling and Immersion
description: A micro-lesson that makes Liquid Cooling and Immersion usable.

hook.text: Microsoft sank a data center to the ocean floor. It ran cooler, failed less, and used zero fresh water.

buildup.text: Air cooling wastes enormous energy. Liquid cooling submerges servers in non-conductive fluid, cutting energy use 40%.

discovery.text: AI workloads generate extreme heat. GPUs push 700 watts each. Liquid cooling is becoming a necessity, not a luxury.

twist.text: Waste heat from data centers can warm nearby buildings. In Finland, data centers already heat homes in winter.

climax.text: The future data center looks nothing like today's. Underwater, underground, or using waste heat to serve the community.

punchline.text: Servers in liquid. Waste heat warms homes. Cooling reinvented.

quiz.question: Why is liquid cooling becoming necessary for data centers?
options: AI workloads generate extreme heat that traditional air cooling can't handle efficiently
Liquid cooling is cheaper than using fans
Air cooling was banned by environmental regulations worldwide
